compiled_state.title: Make sprint-list-epics.sh thin exec wrapper, update tests, add allowlist entry
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## TDD Exemption
Two distinct reasons (both apply):
(a) Change-detector for the exec-delegation assertion: Making sprint-list-epics.sh exec to ticket-list-epics.sh produces zero new observable behavior — exec chain yields identical output, exit codes, and side effects. Any test asserting 'sprint-list-epics.sh now delegates to ticket-list-epics.sh' would be a change-detector (source-grep), not behavioral.
(b) Gate 2 atomicity constraint for test fixture updates: Changing sprint-list-epics.sh to a thin wrapper (sub-step 1) cannot be committed separately from updating tests 28/29/30 (sub-step 2). Tests 28/29/30 copy sprint-list-epics.sh to a temp dir and expect it to be self-contained. After the thin wrapper, sprint-list-epics.sh needs ticket-list-epics.sh alongside it in the temp dir — without this fix, the 3 tests fail, leaving the codebase broken. Gate 2 requires both changes in one commit.

Existing behavioral coverage after this task:
- test-sprint-list-epics.sh tests 1-27, 31-47: pass unchanged (same output via exec chain)
- test-sprint-list-epics.sh tests 28/29/30: pass after fixture update (copy both scripts)
- test-ticket-list-epics-dispatcher.sh 6/6: pass unchanged (dispatcher exercises ticket-list-epics.sh as the canonical entry point)

## Implementation

### Sub-step 1: Replace sprint-list-epics.sh with thin exec wrapper
Replace the full 446-line content of sprint-list-epics.sh with exactly:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
exec "$SCRIPT_DIR/ticket-list-epics.sh" "$@"
```

### Sub-step 2: Update tests 28/29/30 in test-sprint-list-epics.sh
Near the top of the file (after SCRIPT= is defined), add:
  TICKET_LIST_SCRIPT="$DSO_PLUGIN_DIR/scripts/ticket-list-epics.sh"

In test_init_on_missing_tracker (Test 28):
- After: cp "$SCRIPT" "$TDIR28/sprint-list-epics.sh"
- Add:   cp "$TICKET_LIST_SCRIPT" "$TDIR28/ticket-list-epics.sh"

In test_init_skipped_for_override (Test 29):
- After: cp "$SCRIPT" "$TDIR29/sprint-list-epics.sh"
- Add:   cp "$TICKET_LIST_SCRIPT" "$TDIR29/ticket-list-epics.sh"

In test_init_failure_emits_stderr (Test 30):
- After: cp "$SCRIPT" "$TDIR30/sprint-list-epics.sh"
- Add:   cp "$TICKET_LIST_SCRIPT" "$TDIR30/ticket-list-epics.sh"

Note: ticket-init.sh stubs are still created in the same temp dir. ticket-list-epics.sh (the full implementation) uses $SCRIPT_DIR/ticket-init.sh which resolves to the stub in the temp dir. Behavior is preserved.

### Sub-step 3: Add allowlist entry
Add to .claude/hooks/pre-commit/check-tickets-boundary-allowlist.conf (create with minimal header if absent; append if present):
  plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-list-epics.sh  # epic e1b2-2653 — thin wrapper, delegates to ticket-list-epics.sh

### Sub-step 4: Update ticket-cli-reference.md
Find line containing "Thin delegate to `sprint-list-epics.sh`" and replace with:
  "Canonical implementation; `sprint-list-epics.sh` is the thin delegate to this script"

## Files to Modify
- plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-list-epics.sh (replace with 4-line thin exec)
- tests/scripts/test-sprint-list-epics.sh (add ticket-list-epics.sh copy to tests 28/29/30 temp dirs)
- .claude/hooks/pre-commit/check-tickets-boundary-allowlist.conf (add entry; create if absent)
- plugins/dso/docs/ticket-cli-reference.md (update delegation-direction comment)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0) — all 47 sprint-list-epics tests (including updated 28/29/30) pass
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh

- [ ] sprint-list-epics.sh is now a thin wrapper (≤10 lines)
  Verify: lines=$(grep -c '' plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-list-epics.sh); [ "$lines" -le 10 ]

- [ ] sprint-list-epics.sh exec chain preserves 3-valued exit code (exit 1 on empty tracker)
  Verify: TRACKER=$(mktemp -d) && bash plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-list-epics.sh 2>/dev/null; EC=$?; rm -rf "$TRACKER"; [ "$EC" -eq 1 ]

- [ ] sprint-list-epics.sh and ticket-list-epics.sh produce identical output for the same fixture
  Verify: TRACKER=$(mktemp -d) && OUT1=$(TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR="$TRACKER" bash plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-list-epics.sh 2>&1); EC1=$?; OUT2=$(TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR="$TRACKER" bash plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-list-epics.sh 2>&1); EC2=$?; rm -rf "$TRACKER"; [ "$EC1" = "$EC2" ]

- [ ] test-sprint-list-epics.sh tests 28/29/30 individually pass (init-guard behavior preserved)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-sprint-list-epics.sh 2>&1 | grep -q 'Results: 47 passed, 0 failed'

- [ ] sprint-list-epics.sh entry present in boundary allowlist with epic citation
  Verify: grep -F 'sprint-list-epics.sh' .claude/hooks/pre-commit/check-tickets-boundary-allowlist.conf | grep -q 'e1b2-2653'

- [ ] ticket-cli-reference.md updated to reflect reversed delegation direction
  Verify: grep -q 'sprint-list-epics.sh is the thin delegate' plugins/dso/docs/ticket-cli-reference.md

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
